title: Understanding the Problem
text: The shot putter throws the shot straight up. We need to calculate the time it takes for the shot to reach the ground from the release point.

title: Identify the Known Values
text: The initial velocity of the shot is $$11.0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, the release height is $$2.20$$ $$m$$, and the shot putter's height is $$1.80$$ $$m$$.

title: Determine the Total Distance
text: The shot needs to travel from the release height to the ground. Calculate the total distance it needs to fall.

title: Calculate the Total Distance
text: The total distance is the release height minus the shot putter's height. Calculate $$2.20-1.80$$.

title: Use the Kinematic Equation
text: Use the kinematic equation: $$h=v_i t+0.5a t^2$$, where $$h$$ is the height, $$v_i$$ is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration due to gravity $$(-9.8$$ m/s²), and $$t$$ is the time.

title: Solve for Time
text: Rearrange the equation to solve for time: $$0=11t+0.5\left(-9.8\right) t^2-0.4$$. Solve this quadratic equation for $$t$$.
